overview: Grixis Midrange in Pioneer leverages disruptive one-mana spells to control the early game while racing with efficient creatures. By combining card advantage from Thoughtseize with the explosive potential of Flow State, the deck aims to stabilize quickly and outvalue opponents in the mid-to-late game.
win_conditions: Outlasting opponents through superior card advantage and efficient removal
Closing out games with Flow State-empowered creatures
Disrupting opponent's game plan early with Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to stall for resources

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one-mana disruption spells
Hands containing a cheap creature alongside a removal spell
Hands that can play a turn-one Thoughtseize or Fatal Push
mulligan_criteria: Hands with only high-cost creatures and no early interaction
Hands lacking land drops for the first two turns
Hands with multiple Flow States but no creatures to cast

early_game: Play lands and cast one-mana disruption to remove key threats or hands
Establish early board presence with cheap creatures
Use Fatal Push to clear blockers or aggressive threats
mid_game: Cast Flow State to boost your creatures and generate card advantage
Trade efficiently while preserving removal for bigger threats
Apply steady pressure while digging for late-game answers
late_game: Stabilize with resilient creatures and board wipes if necessary
Use Flow State repeatedly to outvalue and close the game
Maintain hand advantage to outlast opponent's resources

key_cards.name: Thoughtseize
key_cards.notes: Use to remove key combo pieces, win conditions, or early threats. Prioritize targeting cards that enable opponent's game plan.

key_cards.name: Flow State
key_cards.notes: Cast on your largest creature to generate value and push damage. Can also be used defensively to clear blockers while drawing a card.

key_cards.name: Fatal Push
key_cards.notes: Use to kill 2-toughness or smaller creatures efficiently. Save for aggressive threats or to clear the way for your attackers.

sequencing_tips: Hold up Fatal Push or Thoughtseize if opponent has a turn-one threat
Cast Flow State before attacking to maximize damage and card draw
Prioritize removing opponent's win conditions over trading creatures early

matchups.advice: Use Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to stall early, then stabilize with Flow State creatures.
watch_for: Pressure before you can cast Flow State

matchups.advice: Race with Flow State creatures while using Thoughtseize to disrupt their late-game plans.
Target key combo pieces with Thoughtseize and use Fatal Push to remove enablers.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype: Grixis Midrange, Format: pioneer, Signature cards: Thoughtseize, Flow State, Fatal Push

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Thoughtseize, Flow State, Fatal Push

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards: Thoughtseize, Flow State, Fatal Push
